funfacts.sentence: Olymp. Dcheira haven't won in their last 8 games.
Renaissance Zemamra's home record this season: 2-4-3.
A year ago, Renaissance Zemamra were number 3 in the table with 34 points. Now they are number 10 with 19 points.

ai_preview.text:
**Renaissance Zemamra are rolling, and Olympique Dcheira are in freefall.** This matchup has "home team takes care of business" written all over it. 🎯

Renaissance just beat Wydad at home and are 3-2 in their last five; Dcheira, meanwhile, are 0-4-1 and leaking goals left and right. The head-to-head also favors the hosts decisively.

Key storylines:

- **Renaissance's attacking form** has them clicking — three wins in five with wins over quality sides like Wydad
- **Dcheira's defensive breakdown** is brutal, losing four of the last five and conceding in every defeat
- **Home dominance** — Renaissance have the edge in familiarity and momentum at their ground

Expected lineups:

- **Renaissance Zemamra**: M. Fakhr, J. Ajako, R. A. Boughima, B. Soufi, A. Badaoui, N. M. Tahiri, F. E. Yazid, A. E. Hamzaoui, A. E. Bajjani, Z. Ghailan, T. Alachbili
- **Olympique Dcheira**: M. Idar, H. A. Brahim, A. Aboujemaa, H. J. Allah, C. Keita, M. E. Gouj, B. Amzili, H. Saoud, Y. Jabrane, A. Benaadi, M. Abba

Best picks:

- **Total Goals 3.5**: Under 3.5 goals

Prediction:

**Renaissance Zemamra 2–0 Olympique Dcheira**. Hosts should cruise past a struggling side. 💪

funfacts.sentence: When playing at home, Gangwon have not lost to Daejeon Citizen in their last 4 encounters.
Lee You-hyeon has more yellow cards (5) than any other player at Gangwon. Cho Sung-gwon has collected 5 for Daejeon Citizen.
A year ago, Daejeon Citizen were number 1 in the table with 24 points. Now they are number 5 with 16 points.

ai_preview.text:
**Gangwon's home turf meets a Daejeon side fresh off a demolition job** — this K League tussle has a bit of everything, and the hosts won't roll over. 🔴

Gangwon's been solid lately with two wins in their last five, though they've drawn their last two at home. Daejeon stormed Gwangju 5-0 last time out but followed it with a dud against Pohang, so consistency is the real test here.

Key storylines:

- **Gangwon FC's defensive shape** will need to be tight; Daejeon just showed they can explode in attack with that 5-0 demolition
- **J. Victor and G. Ludwigson** spearheading Daejeon's attack will be a threat the Gangwon back line (K. Joon-hyuk, M. Tuci, G. Lee) has to respect
- **Gangwon's recent head-to-head edge** gives them a mental boost, but form suggests this is wide open

Expected lineups:

- **Gangwon FC**: C. Park, K. Joon-hyuk, M. Tuci, G. Lee, S. Jun-Seok, D. Kim, Y. Lee, J. H. Mo
- **Daejeon Hana Citizen**: L. Chang-geun, J. Seong-Kwon, M. Kim, M. D. Kim, K. Bong-Soo, V. Bobsin, J. Victor, G. Ludwigson

Prediction:

**Daejeon Hana Citizen 2–1 Gangwon FC**. Model leans Daejeon's way, and that attacking firepower could be the difference on the road. ⚽
